title: crambe fruticosa subsp. pinnatifida
intro: Crambe fruticosa subsp. pinnatifida, also known as the sea kale or orache, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in vitamins and minerals, offering several health benefits such as boosting energy, improving immune function, and supporting eye health. Crambe fruticosa subsp. pinnatifida can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing its medicinal properties to be easily utilized for therapeutic purposes.
